Title: Concanamycins Are Key Contributors to the Virulence of the Potato Common Scab Pathogen Streptomyces scabiei

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enhances virulence of potato common scab pathogen; contributes to seedling necrosis/stunting and potato tuber pitting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: S. scabiei culture extracts; associated with infected radish seedlings and potato tuber tissue symptoms
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of the concanamycin backbone and thus direct production of target metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of conAI abolished concanamycin production and reduced virulence symptoms, especially tissue pitting/necrosis, while thaxtomin A production was unaffected
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; HPLC metabolite profiling; LC-MS; radish seedling bioassay; potato tuber slice bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. conAI encodes the first PKS in the concanamycin BGC. 2. Deletion of conAI eliminated detectable concanamycins in WT and ΔtxtA backgrounds. 3. Loss of conAI reduced S. scabiei virulence, particularly cotyledon necrosis and potato tuber pitting, without affecting thaxtomin A production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth on CPM for concanamycin detection; infection assays on radish seedlings and potato tuber slices
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states the concanamycin backbone is synthesized across six PKS genes conAI-conAVI, but only conAI was directly functionally tested in this study; conAI is the first PKS.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequence FN554889.1 used for BGC identification; bioinformatics with antiSMASH/BiG-SCAPE/cblaster
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates assembly of the concanamycin polyketide backbone as the first module of the multimodular type I PKS system
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for disabling concanamycin production in pathogen-focused control strategies; not a crop improvement gene

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. conAII-conAVI are annotated PKS genes in the concanamycin BGC. 2. They are conserved among concanamycin-like BGCs in several Streptomyces species. 3. They were not individually functionally validated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background/pathway context genes; not direct experimental core subjects in Results.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Elongate and process the concanamycin macrolide backbone
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Possible bldA-dependent post-transcriptional effect on conAII due to one UUA codon mentioned by authors

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enhances virulence on radish seedlings and potato tuber tissue
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extracts; linked to disease lesions in plant tissue
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LAL family (large ATP-binding regulators of the LuxR family)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation within the concanamycin BGC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of conR1 abolished detectable concanamycin production; complementation restored production; conR1 acts upstream of conR2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential positive activator required for concanamycin production
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates expression of concanamycin biosynthetic genes, likely partly via conR2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: conR2; concanamycin biosynthetic genes in the BGC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted deletion; complementation with ermEp*-driven conR1; cross-complementation; overexpression; HPLC metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. conR1 is a cluster-situated LAL-family regulator in the concanamycin BGC. 2. Deletion of conR1 eliminated detectable concanamycins. 3. Complementation with conR1 restored production, while conR2 overexpression could bypass conR1 loss, indicating conR1 is likely above conR2 in the regulatory cascade.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: possibly global developmental regulators BldC/BldD/BldG upstream of pathway regulation; direct control of conR1 not yet shown
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ConR2; BldA (post-transcriptional dependency inferred from UUA codons)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth on concanamycin production medium (CPM) agar
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains four UUA codons, suggesting likely post-transcriptional regulation by bldA tRNA; this was proposed but not directly tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequence FN554889.1 used for BGC analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ermEp* constitutive promoter used for complementation/overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target to suppress pathogen virulence by blocking concanamycin biosynthesis

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: enhances virulence on radish seedlings and potato tuber tissue
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extracts; associated with potato tuber pitting and seedling necrosis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of conR2 abolished detectable concanamycin production; complementation restored production; overexpression increased concanamycin production and enhanced virulence symptoms
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key positive activator and effective overproduction switch for concanamycin output
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates transcription of concanamycin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: concanamycin biosynthetic genes in the BGC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted deletion; complementation with ermEp*-driven conR2; overexpression in WT and ΔtxtA; cross-complementation; HPLC metabolite profiling; radish seedling bioassay; potato tuber slice bioassay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. conR2 is a cluster-situated SARP-family activator essential for concanamycin biosynthesis. 2. Deletion of conR2 eliminated detectable concanamycins, while complementation restored production. 3. Overexpression of conR2 increased concanamycin accumulation and enhanced virulence, and could rescue the ΔconR1 mutant, placing conR2 downstream of conR1.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: expression previously reported to be activated by global regulators BldC; BldD; BldG
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BldC; BldD; BldG; ConR1; BldA (post-transcriptional dependency inferred from UUA codon)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth on CPM agar for metabolite assays; infection assays on radish seedlings and potato tuber slices
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains one UUA codon, suggesting likely bldA-dependent post-transcriptional regulation. Overexpression did not alter thaxtomin A production in WT background.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No reported effect on thaxtomin A levels in WT background; broader physiological costs not reported
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: genome sequence FN554889.1 used for BGC analysis
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ermEp* constitutive promoter used for complementation/overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong leverage point for engineering concanamycin overproduction or for anti-virulence suppression of the pathogen
